item.title: C# 编程简介 | Introduction to C# Programming
item.description:
公共语言运行时 (CLR)。

  • 类库。

    • 它提供诸如内存管理、线程管理、类型安全、安全和异常处理等服务。 执行 C# 代码时，它不会转换为机器本机语言； 相反，它被转换为一种称为 CIL（通用中间语言）的中间语言，它独立于运行它的机器。 然后，CLR 通过称为 JIT（即时）编译的过程将 CIL 翻译成计算机可以理解的本地语言。 这种架构消除了将您的应用程序转换为不同机器的本地语言的需要。

    类库

    类库是称为类的代码块，它们在运行时协同工作，为应用程序提供功能。 类是一个容器，其中包含一些称为属性和函数的数据，也称为方法。 一个类可能包含许多功能，每个功能都设计用于执行应用程序中的特定任务。 例如，您可以有一个用于数据库、安全、图形等的命名空间。C# 命名空间然后组织在一个程序集中，它是一个命名空间的容器。 程序集也称为动态链接库 (DLL)。

    变量是赋予内存中存储位置的名称，而常量是不可变的值（主要用于创建安全性）。

    变量声明

    声明变量时，我们从类型开始，然后是标识符，最后是分号。

    例如：

    在上面的示例中，“int”是 C# 数据类型（整数），而“number”是标识符。 有时，您可能需要给变量一个值。

    例如：

    这称为实例化。 每个变量都需要先实例化，然后才能在应用程序中使用。

    不能以数字开头。 例如，int 4number。 相反，使用：int fournumber。

  • 不能包含空格。

    • 例如：

    相反，使用：

    例如：

    相反，使用：

    在命名标识符时使用有意义的名称很重要。

    例如： ，在命名字符串时，
    使用：

    代替：

    命名约定

    注意外壳。 一、骆驼案：

    例如：

    二。 帕斯卡案例：

    例如：

    一、整数

    二。 实数

    三、 人物

    整数包括：

    实数包括：

    字符包括：

    非原始类型

    它们包括以下内容：

    一、字符串

    二、数组

    三、 枚举

    四、 班级

    构建桌面应用程序。

  • 与Unity游戏引擎一起使用的游戏开发。
  • 移动应用程序的开发。



    • 原文:

    C# is a high-level programming language developed by Microsoft as one of the languages in the .NET framework. Other languages in .NET include F# and VB.NET. .NET provides an infrastructure on which these languages can be used to develop applications that run on various platforms like Windows, Linux, and macOS.

    Architecture of .NET Applications

    .NET consists of two main components:

    1. The Common Language Runtime (CLR).
    2. Class Libraries.

    CLR (Common Language Runtime)

    CLR is a runtime environment responsible for executing managed code written in any of the .NET framework languages, including C#. It offers services such as memory management, thread management, type safety, security, and exception handling. When a C# code is executed, it is not converted to machine-native language; instead, it is converted to an intermediate language known as CIL (Common Intermediate Language), which is independent of the machine it is running on. Then, CLR translates CIL to a native language, which a computer can understand, through a process called JIT (Just-In-Time) compilation. This architecture eliminates the need to convert your app into native languages for different machines.

    Class Libraries

    Class libraries are blocks of codes called classes that work together at runtime, giving the application its functionalities. A class is a container with some data called attributes and functions also called methods. A class may contain many functions, each designed to perform specific tasks in the application. C# classes are organized and contained in namespaces, which group related classes performing different tasks. For example, you can have a namespace for databases, security, graphics, etc. The C# namespaces are then organized in an assembly, which is a container of namespaces. An assembly is also referred to as a Dynamically Linked Library (DLL).

    C# Variables and Constants

    A variable is a name given to a storage location in memory, while a constant is an immutable value (mostly used to create safety).

    Variable Declaration

    When declaring a variable, we start with a type followed by an identifier and finally a semicolon.

    For Example:

    In the example above, "int" is a C# data type (integer), while "number" is the identifier. At times, you may need to give a variable a value.

    For Example:

    This is called instantiation. Every variable needs to be instantiated before it can be used in an application.

    C# Identifier Don'ts

    C# language doesn't allow the following ways of naming identifiers:

    • Cannot start with a number. For example, int 4number. Instead, use: int fournumber.
    • Cannot include whitespace.

    For Example:

    Instead, use:

    For Example:

    Instead, use:

    It is important to use meaningful names as you name identifiers.

    For Example:, when naming a string,
    use:

    instead of:

    Naming Conventions

    Note the casing. There are two types of naming conventions used in C#:

    I. Camel case:

    For Example:

    II. Pascal Case:

    For Example:

    C# Primitive Types

    .NET has the following primitive types:

    I. Integral numbers

    II. Real numbers

    III. Characters

    Integral numbers include:

    Real numbers include:

    Characters include:

    Non-primitive types

    They include the following:

    I. String

    II.Array

    III. Enum

    IV. Class

    Uses of C#

    • Backend development of web applications and services.
    • Building desktop applications.
    • Game development used together with Unity game Engine.
    • Development of mobile applications.

hi everyone i am trying to add my currency on alchemy pay platform and i am facing a problem with the scenario
i made a price query api first as per this page alchemypay.readme.io/docs/price-query-1 user choose amount and fiat and click pay -> fiat currency is paid from user credit card

-> alchemy pay notify me that the user paid (so i created a web hook to receive that as per this page alchemypay.readme.io/docs/webhook-notif…)

-> i transfer the crypto amount to the user wallet (the logic for this is inside the notification api i just created)

-> says should be 1 more notification but can't understand also there is this page which i don't know should i implement it or not alchemypay.readme.io/docs/callback

item.title: 适用于电子商务业务的 Akeneo ERP：与 Magento 2 的平滑集成 | Akeneo ERP for eCommerce Business: Smooth Integration with Magento 2
item.description:
但在我们深入了解这种集成的细节之前，让我们仔细看看 Akeneo 及其在电子商务世界中的重要性。

随着在线购物越来越受欢迎，企业拥有强大的产品信息管理系统变得越来越重要。 在本文中，我们将探讨将 Akeneo 用于您的电子商务网站的好处，以及适用于 Magento 2 的 Akeneo 连接器如何提高您的 eShop 的效率。 那么，让我们开始吧。

什么是 Akeneo？

该系统具有灵活性和可扩展性，使各种规模的企业都能根据自己的需要进行定制。

产品资料

同时，其先进的工作流功能使团队能够轻松高效地协作处理产品数据。

此外，Akeneo 的高级数据丰富功能确保所有产品信息在所有销售渠道中都是准确、最新和一致的。 这可以改善客户体验并增加销售额。

产品页面

它管理多语言产品描述、图像和视频的能力使企业能够创建更具吸引力和信息量更大的产品页面。

运营效率

根据 Forrester 的一项研究，使用 Akeneo 的公司在产品信息管理上花费的时间减少了 55%，在线销售额增加了 40%。 这表明 Akeneo 在推动销售增长的同时显着提高了电子商务业务的运营效率。

多渠道销售

企业经常选择混合线上和线下销售，而不是仅仅依靠他们的电子商务平台来赚钱。 需要一个平台，让您可以从一个位置管理所有渠道，无论您是进入新市场、发布印刷目录还是计划广告活动。

完工监测

迎合国际市场通常需要以多种语言发布内容并仔细监控产品数据的正确性。 扩大规模总是需要大部分这些活动的自动化。

一致的客户体验

根据埃森哲的一项调查，61% 的客户希望在与品牌互动时通过所有渠道获得一致的体验。 借助 Akeneo，电子商务企业可以提供一致且个性化的客户体验，从而提高客户满意度和忠诚度。

商店产品目录的质量对数字商务的两个关键方面有影响：

  • 流量生成：通过提供有用的材料，您可以吸引认真购买的用户并提高您的搜索引擎评级。
  • 产品转化率：您可以通过提供有助于推动、激发和鼓励购买的详细且有吸引力的内容来实现更好的销售量。

总之，Akeneo 是一个功能强大的 PIM 系统，可为电子商务企业提供许多功能和优势。 它的灵活性、可扩展性和高级功能使其成为任何希望改进产品数据管理和推动电子商务成功的企业的必备工具。

借助 Akeneo，企业可以简化产品数据管理，提高数据准确性和一致性，并增强产品页面，从而提高运营效率，提高客户满意度，并最终增加销售额。

如果您从事电子商务业务，就会明白管理产品信息可能是一项艰巨的任务。 尤其是当您考虑到必须管理的产品和渠道的数量时，很快就会变得不知所措。

这就是产品信息管理 (PIM) 软件的用武之地。PIM 软件简化了管理产品信息的过程，使您能够有效地跨多个渠道管理产品数据。

它旨在帮助各种规模的电子商务企业简化其产品数据管理流程，无论他们必须管理的产品或渠道数量如何。

无论您拥有小型在线商店还是大型电子商务平台，Akeneo 都可以帮助您改进产品数据管理流程，显着影响您的整体业务效率。

通过将 Akeneo 强大的 PIM 功能与 Magento 2 强大的电子商务平台相集成，该连接器提供了一系列可以提高效率、增加销售额和增强客户体验的优势。 以下是一些主要优势：

  1. 集中的产品信息管理：使用Akeneo，您可以轻松地在一个地方管理和丰富您的产品数据。 这种集中式方法使您能够确保跨渠道的一致性，减少错误和冗余，并改善团队成员之间的协作。
  2. 强大的产品数据丰富：Akeneo 的 PIM 系统提供一系列工具和功能，使您能够以有意义的方式丰富您的产品数据。 您可以添加和管理产品属性、创建关系以及轻松地将数据导入和导出其他系统。
  3. 更快的上市时间：Akeneo 可以通过简化您的产品信息管理流程，帮助您更快地将新产品推向市场。 花在手动数据输入上的时间更少，将更多时间专注于战略产品开发，您可以缩短上市时间并获得竞争优势。
  4. 改善客户体验：通过跨渠道准确、最新的产品信息，您可以为客户提供更好的购物体验。 客户可以轻松找到他们想要的产品，访问详细信息和图像，并做出明智的购买决定。
  5. 增加销售和收入：优化产品信息管理可以通过多种方式提高销售和收入。 例如，您可以通过提供准确全面的产品信息来减少购物车放弃率，更有效地追加销售和交叉销售产品，并通过提供更好的购物体验来提高客户保留率。

为什么我们推荐 Akeneo PIM + Magento 2：

  • 它为目录管理提供了更好的用户体验。
  • 它确保每个区域设置的内容完整性所需的可见性。
  • 它有助于减少 Magento 目录中的产品数量。 这通过允许 Magento 仅存储其项目的一个子集而其余部分存储在 PIM 中直到它们准备好被激活来提高性能。
  • 由于 Akeneo 支持多种渠道，因此同时拥有实体店和在线商店的卖家可以在同一屋檐下处理每个渠道的产品信息。
  • 它使产品经理能够仅通过 PIM 处理目录，无需授权访问 Magento 2 后端。
  • 它可以作为产品导入源的中心，如果您的公司与多个供应商打交道，这将非常有用。

为 Magento 2 使用 Akeneo 连接器的潜在缺陷：

  1. 初始设置和配置可能既复杂又耗时。
  2. 持续的维护和管理可能需要大量资源。
  3. 新用户的学习曲线可能很陡峭。
  4. 实施和维护的成本可能很高。
  5. 与其他第三方工具和扩展的集成可能需要额外的开发工作。

然而，这些挑战可以通过适当的支持和专业知识来克服，并且系统的好处可以远远超过任何潜在的缺点。

集成 Akeneo PIM 和 Magento 2 可以为电子商务企业带来许多好处。 通过集中产品信息和简化系统间的数据流，Magento 2 Akeneo 连接器可以显着减少管理大量产品目录的工作量。

将 Akeneo PIM 与 Magento 2 集成

要将 Akeneo PIM 与 Magento 2 集成，您可以使用 Magento 2 Akeneo 连接器，这是一个免费的开源扩展，可无缝集成两个平台。 集成过程包括以下步骤：

  1. 在您的 Magento 2 实例上安装 Magento 2 Akeneo 连接器扩展。
  2. 通过提供 Akeneo PIM 实例的 API 凭证配置 Akeneo PIM 和 Magento 2 之间的连接。
  3. 在 Akeneo PIM 和 Magento 2 之间映射产品属性和类别。
  4. 将产品数据从 Akeneo PIM 导入到 Magento 2。

管理 Akeneo PIM + Magento 2 集成的专业技巧

要充分利用 Akeneo PIM + Magento 2 集成，请考虑实施以下提示：

  • 在将其导入 Magento 2 之前，确保 Akeneo PIM 中的产品数据准确且最新。
  • 仔细映射 Akeneo PIM 和 Magento 2 之间的产品属性和类别，以避免数据不一致。
  • 在将产品数据导入 Magento 2 之前，使用 Akeneo PIM 数据质量工具确保产品数据符合要求的标准。
  • 定期监控 Akeneo PIM 和 Magento 2 之间的数据流，以确保产品信息保持一致和最新。
  • 为进一步确保产品数据的质量，请在 Akeneo PIM 中设置数据验证规则。
  • 定期在 Akeneo PIM 和 Magento 2 之间同步数据，以确保您的商店始终掌握最新信息。
  • 确保您的团队成员接受过使用 Akeneo PIM 和 Magento 2 的培训。
  • 使用分析工具来跟踪产品的性能并深入了解客户行为。
  • 建立明确的数据治理政策和流程来保护您的产品数据。

您应该使用哪个 Akeneo 连接器版本？

目前有两个版本的 Akeneo Connector for Magento 2：开源版和企业版。

免费的开源版本提供了基本的集成功能，例如产品导入/导出和属性映射。 但是，可能需要额外的开发工作来根据特定业务需求定制集成。

另一方面，企业版提供更多高级功能，例如实时同步、高级映射规则和增强的安全性。 它还包括专业的支持和维护服务，以确保平稳运行并最大限度地减少停机时间。 但是，企业版需要支付许可费，这可能仅适用于某些企业。

选择要使用的 Akeneo 连接器版本时，请考虑以下因素：

  1. 兼容性：连接器必须与您的 Akeneo PIM 和目标系统版本兼容。
  2. 特性和功能：选择具有适合您业务需求的特性和功能的连接器。
  3. 性能和可靠性：连接器必须性能可靠，以确保系统之间的数据传输顺畅。
  4. 支持和维护：连接器应提供定期更新和修复以确保最佳性能。

建议在做出最终决定之前，根据这些因素比较不同的连接器。

如果您的资源有限并且需要基本的集成功能，开源版本可能是一个不错的选择。 但是，如果您有复杂的集成需求并需要专业的支持和维护，企业版可能会更好。

概括

通过集中产品信息和自动执行重复性任务，公司可以提高运营效率并专注于更具战略意义的计划。 总的来说，该系统的好处可以大大超过潜在的缺陷，从而提高运营效率、增加销售额并增强客户体验。

据 Akeneo 称，使用 Akeneo 的企业管理产品数据的时间减少了 30%，转化率提高了 50%。 无论您选择开源版还是企业版，该系统的灵活性和可扩展性都使其成为任何希望推动电子商务成功的企业的必备工具。



原文:

The Akeneo Connector for Magento 2 is a powerful integration that allows eCommerce businesses to manage their product data more efficiently and effectively. But before we dive into the details of this integration, let’s take a closer look at Akeneo and its importance in the eCommerce world.

As online shopping grows in popularity, it’s becoming increasingly crucial for businesses to have a robust product information management system. Akeneo provides just that, with key features such as centralised data management, easy data enrichment, and robust API capabilities.

In this article, we’ll explore the benefits of using Akeneo for your eCommerce website and how the Akeneo Connector for Magento 2 can enhance the effectiveness of your eShop. So, let’s get started.

What Is Akeneo?

Akeneo is a robust Product Information Management (PIM) system that centralises product data from multiple sources, such as suppliers, manufacturers, and distributors, into a single location. This system offers flexibility and scalability, enabling businesses of all sizes to customise it according to their needs.

Product Data

Akeneo reviews agree that it has an intuitive interface that simplifies product information creation, editing, and management. At the same time, its advanced workflow capabilities make it easy for teams to collaborate on product data efficiently.

Moreover, Akeneo’s advanced data enrichment capabilities ensure that all product information is accurate, up-to-date, and consistent across all sales channels. This leads to an improved customer experience and increased sales.

Product Pages

Akeneo offers significant value for eCommerce websites by providing a reliable and centralised product data source. Its ability to manage multilingual product descriptions, images, and videos enables businesses to create more engaging and informative product pages.

Operational Efficiency

According to a study by Forrester, companies using Akeneo have seen a 55% reduction in time spent on product information management and a 40% increase in online sales. This shows that Akeneo significantly improves the operational efficiency of eCommerce businesses while driving sales growth.

Sales via Several Channels

Businesses frequently choose to mix online and offline sales rather than depending solely on their eCommerce platforms for earnings. There is a need for a platform that allows you to manage all of your channels from one location, whether you are entering a new market, publishing a printed catalogue, or planning an ad campaign.

Completion Monitoring

Catering to international markets frequently necessitates releasing content in many languages and carefully monitoring product data correctness. Scaling up invariably requires the automation of a large portion of these activities.

Consistent Experience for Customers

According to a survey by Accenture, 61% of customers expect consistent experiences across all channels when interacting with a brand. With Akeneo, eCommerce businesses can provide a consistent and personalised customer experience, increasing customer satisfaction and loyalty.

The quality of your store’s product catalogue has an impact on two critical aspects of digital commerce:

  • Traffic generation: By providing useful material, you can attract users serious about making a purchase and improve your search engine rating.
  • Product conversion: you can achieve better sales volumes by providing detailed and appealing content that helps drive, excite, and encourage the buy.

In summary, Akeneo is a powerful PIM system offering many features and benefits to eCommerce businesses. Its flexibility, scalability, and advanced capabilities make it an essential tool for any business looking to improve product data management and drive eCommerce success.

With Akeneo, businesses can streamline product data management, improve data accuracy and consistency, and enhance product pages, leading to improved operational efficiency, increased customer satisfaction, and, ultimately, more sales.

Who Is PIM & Akeneo For?

If you’re in the eCommerce business, you understand that managing product information can be daunting. Especially when you consider the number of products and channels you have to manage, it can quickly become overwhelming.

This is where Product Information Management (PIM) software comes in. PIM software streamlines the process of managing product information, allowing you to manage your product data across multiple channels efficiently.

Akeneo Magento 2 Connector is a leading PIM software that provides a comprehensive solution for managing product information. It is designed to help eCommerce businesses of all sizes streamline their product data management process, regardless of the number of products or channels they have to manage.

Whether you have a small online store or a large eCommerce platform, Akeneo can help you improve your product data management process, significantly impacting your overall business effectiveness.

Key Benefits of Akeneo PIM + Magento 2

By integrating Akeneo’s powerful PIM capabilities with Magento 2’s robust eCommerce platform, this connector provides a range of benefits that can improve efficiency, increase sales, and enhance customer experience. Here are some of the key benefits:

  1. Centralised product information management: With Akeneo, you can easily manage and enrich your product data in one place. This centralised approach allows you to ensure consistency across channels, reduce errors and redundancies, and improve collaboration among team members.
  2. Robust product data enrichment: Akeneo’s PIM system offers a range of tools and features that allow you to enrich your product data in meaningful ways. You can add and manage product attributes, create relationships, and easily import and export data to and from other systems.
  3. Faster time-to-market: Akeneo can help you get new products to market faster by streamlining your product information management processes. With less time spent on manual data entry and more time focused on strategic product development, you can improve your time-to-market and gain a competitive edge.
  4. Improved customer experience: With accurate, up-to-date product information across channels, you can provide customers with a better shopping experience. Customers can easily find their desired products, access detailed information and images, and make informed purchasing decisions.
  5. Increased sales and revenue: Optimizing your product information management can improve sales and revenue in several ways. For example, you can reduce cart abandonment by providing accurate and comprehensive product information, upsell and cross-sell products more effectively, and improve customer retention by providing a better shopping experience.

Why We Recommend Akeneo PIM + Magento 2:

  • It provides a better user experience for catalogue management.
  • It ensures the visibility required for content completeness per locale.
  • It aids in reducing the number of products in the Magento catalogue. This improves performance by allowing Magento to store only a subset of its items while the rest are stored in the PIM until they are ready to be activated.
  • Because Akeneo supports numerous channels, a seller with both physical and online stores may handle product information for each under the same roof.
  • It enables product managers to deal with the catalogue solely through the PIM, eliminating the need to authorise access to the Magento 2 back-end.
  • It can serve as a central centre for product import feeds, which is useful if your company deals with multiple suppliers.

Potential Pitfalls of Using Akeneo Connector for Magento 2:

  1. The initial setup and configuration can be complex and time-consuming.
  2. Ongoing maintenance and management may require significant resources.
  3. The learning curve for new users can be steep.
  4. The cost of implementation and maintenance can be high.
  5. The integration with other third-party tools and extensions may require additional development work.

However, these challenges can be overcome with the proper support and expertise, and the system’s benefits can far outweigh any potential drawbacks.

Akeneo PIM + Magento 2 Integration

Integrating Akeneo PIM and Magento 2 can provide numerous benefits for eCommerce businesses. By centralising product information and streamlining the data flow between systems, the Magento 2 Akeneo Connector can significantly reduce the workload of managing large catalogues of products.

Integrating Akeneo PIM with Magento 2

To integrate Akeneo PIM with Magento 2, you can use the Magento 2 Akeneo Connector, a free, open-source extension that seamlessly integrates the two platforms. The integration process involves the following steps:

  1. Install the Magento 2 Akeneo Connector extension on your Magento 2 instance.
  2. Configure the connection between Akeneo PIM and Magento 2 by providing the API credentials of your Akeneo PIM instance.
  3. Map the product attributes and categories between Akeneo PIM and Magento 2.
  4. Import the product data from Akeneo PIM to Magento 2.

Pro Tips for Managing Akeneo PIM + Magento 2 Integration

To get the most out of the Akeneo PIM + Magento 2 integration, consider implementing the following tips:

  • Ensure the product data in Akeneo PIM is accurate and up-to-date before importing it into Magento 2.
  • Carefully map the product attributes and categories between Akeneo PIM and Magento 2 to avoid data inconsistencies.
  • Use the Akeneo PIM data quality tools to ensure the product data meets the required standards before importing it into Magento 2.
  • Regularly monitor the data flow between Akeneo PIM and Magento 2 to ensure the product information remains consistent and up-to-date.
  • To further ensure the quality of your product data, set up data validation rules within Akeneo PIM.
  • Regularly sync data between Akeneo PIM and Magento 2 to ensure your store is always up-to-date with the latest information.
  • Ensure your team members are trained to use Akeneo PIM and Magento 2.
  • Use analytics tools to track the performance of your products and gain insights into customer behaviour.
  • Establish clear data governance policies and processes to secure your product data.

Which Akeneo Connector Version Should You Use?

There are currently two versions of the Akeneo Connector for Magento 2: open-source and enterprise.

The free, open-source version provides basic integration features like product import/export and attribute mapping. However, it may require additional development efforts to customise the integration to specific business needs.

The enterprise version, on the other hand, offers more advanced features, such as real-time synchronisation, advanced mapping rules, and enhanced security. It also includes professional support and maintenance services to ensure smooth operation and minimise downtime. However, the enterprise version comes with a licensing fee, which may only be feasible for some businesses.

When choosing which Akeneo connector version to use, consider the following factors:

  1. Compatibility: The connector must be compatible with your Akeneo PIM and target system versions.
  2. Features and functionalities: Choose a connector with the features and functionalities that suit your business requirements.
  3. Performance and reliability: The connector must be performant and reliable to ensure smooth data transfer between systems.
  4. Support and maintenance: The connector should provide regular updates and fixes to ensure optimal performance.

Comparing different connectors based on these factors is recommended before making a final decision.

The open-source version may be a good option if you have limited resources and require basic integration features. However, the enterprise version may be better if you have complex integration needs and require professional support and maintenance.

Summary

The Akeneo Magento 2 connector is a powerful tool to help eCommerce businesses streamline their product information management and enhance customer experience. By centralising product information and automating repetitive tasks, companies can achieve operational efficiency and focus on more strategic initiatives. Overall, the system’s benefits can significantly outweigh the potential pitfalls, resulting in improved operational efficiency, increased sales, and enhanced customer experience.

According to Akeneo, businesses that use Akeneo have experienced a 30% reduction in time spent managing product data and up to a 50% increase in conversion rates. Whether you choose the open-source or enterprise version, the system’s flexibility and scalability make it an essential tool for any business looking to drive eCommerce success.
item.author: vyacheslavkonyshev

item.title: 使用带有示例的 Linux Free 命令 | Using the Linux Free Command With Examples
item.description:
我写这篇文章最初是为了图灵

定期内存检查对于维护 Linux 系统的性能至关重要。 了解系统的内存还可以帮助调试和防止在运行内存密集型程序时应用程序响应时间不佳。

有多种方法可以使用命令行查看 Linux 系统上的 RAM，但就本文的范围而言，我们将重点关注“free”命令。

在本文中，我们将讨论什么是 Linux free 命令、它的工作原理以及该命令的好处。

什么是free命令？

要查看系统的内存统计信息，请在终端中运行 free 命令，如下所示：

 user@ubuntu:~$ free total used free shared buff/cache available Mem: 8029356 794336 6297928 183384 937092 6816804 Swap: 0 0 0

了解输出

要全面了解这些领域，逐一考察非常重要。 首先，我们将关注“内存”指标，它表示系统上安装的物理 RAM 总量。

检查Mem指标及其字段

在free命令的输出中显示的Mem指标用作计算机系统上安装的物理 RAM（随机存取存储器）的度量。 它通过运行程序和进程提供已安装 RAM 总量及其当前使用情况的综合视图。 通过监视此指标，可以检测系统中任何潜在的与内存相关的问题。

总计：这是系统上物理 RAM 的总量。

  • 它用于通过减少从硬盘驱动器访问数据所需的时间来加快计算机的性能。 可以把它想象成一个临时存储区，计算机可以在其中存储可能很快需要的数据，这样以后就不必再次搜索了。

  • 可用：这显示了仍有多少内存资源可供使用的估计值。 该值会随着进程的启动和停止以及内存的释放和分配而波动。 因此，虽然它目前可能没有被进程主动使用，但如果需要，它仍然可以分配给进程。

    • 空闲和可用内存可能很难理解。 将空闲内存想象成您家中随时可以被占用的空房间，代表当前未被任何正在运行的进程使用并准备分配给新进程的物理内存量。 您还可以将可用内存视为可以占用的房间总数，包括空房间和用于缓存和缓冲的房间。

    缓存的一个示例是将项目存储在您经常使用的储藏室中，以便您可以在需要时轻松访问它们。 缓冲就像准备好客房以备不时之需。 储藏室和客房都在使用中，但它们仍然被认为是“可用的”，因为它们可以在必要时用于预期目的。

    检查swap指标及其字段

    交换指标显示当前正在使用的交换空间量和可供使用的交换空间量。

    交换，也称为虚拟内存，是一种使计算机系统能够通过在存储卷上创建文件或分区来使用额外内存的机制。 当系统的物理 RAM 已满且无法容纳新进程时，这用作备份选项。 操作系统将数据从 RAM 传输到交换空间，使系统继续平稳运行。

    回顾运行 free 命令时获得的先前输出，swap 的输出字段结果为零 (0)。 这意味着系统上没有可用的交换空间。

    自由命令选项

    要以字节为单位获取内存信息，请在命令中添加-b选项

    user@ubuntu:~$ free -b total used free sharedbuff/cache available Mem: 8222060544 700334080 6214823936 188076032 1306902528 7087677440 Swap: 0 0 0
    • 要获取以千字节为单位的内存信息，请将-k选项添加到命令中。

    单独使用 free 命令时，如前所述，不带任何选项，默认显示的内存以千字节为单位。

    • 要查看以兆字节为单位的内存信息，请在命令中添加-m选项
    user@ubuntu:~$ free -m total used free shared buff/cache available Mem: 7841 668 5925 179 1247 6759 Swap: 0 0 0
    • 要查看以千兆字节为单位的内存信息，请在命令中添加-g选项
    user@ubuntu:~$ free -g total used free shared buff/cache available Mem: 7 0 5 0 1 6 Swap: 0 0 0
    • 要以人类可读的格式查看内存信息，请在命令中添加-h选项
    user@ubuntu:~$ free -h total used free shared buff/cache available Mem: 7.7Gi 675Mi 5.8Gi 179Mi 1.2Gi 6.6Gi Swap: 0B 0B 0B

    在本节中，我们将深入探讨使用此命令的好处。

    1. 显示当前内存使用情况
      运行不带任何参数的 free 命令将显示系统上当前已用和可用内存量，以及用于系统缓冲区和磁盘缓存的内存量。 这有助于您了解占用系统存储空间的进程。

    2. 监控一段时间内的内存使用情况
      通过结合使用 free 命令和 watch 命令，您可以定期显示当前内存使用情况。 例如，“watch -n 5 free -m”将每 5 秒显示一次当前内存使用情况。 这对于识别内存使用随时间变化的模式很有用。

    3. 识别内存泄漏
      如果 free 命令输出的“used”列一直很高，则可能表明其中一个正在运行的程序存在内存泄漏。 通过定期运行该命令并检查已用内存，您可以确定导致泄漏的进程。

    4. 检查高缓冲区/缓存使用率
      如果“buffers”和“cached”列一直很高，则可能表明系统正在使用大量内存进行缓存。 虽然这通常不是问题，但如果系统内存不足，它可能会导致性能下降。

    5. 以不同单位显示内存使用情况
      通过使用 -m 或 -g 选项，您可以分别以兆字节或千兆字节显示内存使用情况。

    6. 与其他命令行工具的比较
      结论

    在本文中，我们讨论了 free 命令、它的工作原理和好处。

    了解 free 命令的输出可以帮助管理员和用户识别潜在的内存瓶颈并解决性能问题。



    原文:

    I wrote this article originally for Turing

    Regular memory checks are crucial to maintaining the performance of your Linux system. Knowing your system's memory can also aid in debugging and preventing poor application response times while running memory-intensive programs.

    There are several ways to view the RAM on your Linux system using the command line, but for the scope of this article, we will focus on the 'free' command.

    In this article, we will discuss what the Linux free command is, how it works, and the benefits of the command.

    What is the free command?

    The free command is a Linux command that allows you to check for memory RAM on your system or to check the memory statics of the Linux operating system.

    To view your system's memory statics, run the free command in your terminal as shown below:

    user@ubuntu:~$ free

          total    used      free   shared   buff/cache  available
Mem:    8029356  794336   6297928   183384       937092    6816804
Swap:         0       0         0

    Understanding the output

    The output of the free command provides various metrics related to system memory, including total, used, free, shared, buff/cache, and available. To gain a comprehensive understanding of these fields, it is important to examine each one individually. To begin, we'll focus on the 'Mem' metric, which represents the total amount of physical RAM installed on the system.

    Examining the Mem metrics and its fields

    The Mem metric, as displayed in the output of the free command, serves as a measurement of the physical RAM (Random Access Memory) installed on a computer system. It offers a comprehensive view of the total amount of installed RAM and its current usage by running programs and processes. By monitoring this metric, it is possible to detect any potential memory-related issues within the system.

    The Mem metric also includes several fields that give an overview of the system's memory usage, such as:

    1. Total: This is the total amount of physical RAM on your system.

    2. Used: This shows the amount of memory that has been used up or amount of RAM that is currently being utilized by running programs and processes.

    3. Free: This is the amount of physical memory that is not currently being used by any running processes and is ready to be allocated to new processes.

    4. Shared: This displays the total amount of memory used by the temporary tmpfs file system. Tmpfs is a file system that stores files in the computer's main memory (RAM) making it faster to access compared to traditional storage methods like a hard drive.

    5. Buff/cache: This is the memory that the kernel (operating system) uses to store recently used data so that it can be accessed quickly. It is used to speed up the performance of the computer by reducing the amount of time it takes to access data from the hard drive. Think of it like a temporary storage area where the computer stores data that it might need soon, so that it doesn't have to search for it again later.

    6. Available: This shows an estimated value of how many memory resources are still open for use. This value can fluctuate as processes start and stop, and memory is freed up and allocated. So, while it may not actively be used by a process at the moment, it is still available to be allocated to a process if needed.

    Free and available memory can be tricky to understand. Think of free memory as empty rooms in your house that are ready to be occupied, representing the amount of physical memory that is not currently being used by any running processes and is ready to be allocated to new processes. You can also think of available memory as the total number of rooms that can be occupied including the empty ones and the ones in use for caching and buffering.

    An example of caching would be storing items in a storage room that you frequently use, so that you can easily access them when you need them. Buffering would be like having a guest room ready to be used in case you have unexpected visitors. Both the storage room and guest room are being used, but they are still considered "available" because they can be used for their intended purpose if necessary.

    Examining the swap metrics and its fields

    The swap metric shows the amount of swap space that is currently being used and the amount of swap space that is available for use.

    Swap, also known as virtual memory, is a mechanism that enables computer systems to use extra memory by creating a file or partition on a storage volume. This serves as a backup option when the system's physical RAM is full and can't accommodate new processes. The operating system transfers data from RAM to the swap space, allowing the system to continue running smoothly.

    The swap metric also includes several fields that give an overview of the system's memory usage, such as:

    1. total: The size of the swap partition or swap file
    2. used: The amount of swap space in use
    3. free: The remaining (unused) swap space

    Referring back to the previous output gotten when the free command was run, the output fields for swap resulted as zero (0). This entails that there is no swap space made available on the system.

    Free command options

    The free command can be tailored to show memory usage in any desired format.

    • To get the memory information in bytes, add the -b option to the command 
    user@ubuntu:~$ free -b

             total         used         free      sharedbuff/cache     
  available
Mem:    8222060544    700334080   6214823936   188076032     1306902528    7087677440
Swap:         0       0         0
    • To get the memory information in kilobytes, add the -k option to the command.

    When the free command is used alone, as was demonstrated earlier, without any option, the default displayed of the memory is in kilobytes.

    • To view the memory information in megabytes, add the -m option to the command 
    user@ubuntu:~$ free -m

          total    used    free   shared    buff/cache   available
Mem:       7841     668    5925      179          1247        6759
Swap:         0       0         0
    • To view the memory information in gigabytes, add the -g option to the command 
    user@ubuntu:~$ free -g

          total    used    free   shared    buff/cache   available
Mem:          7       0       5        0             1           6
Swap:         0       0       0
    • To view the memory information in human-readeable format, add the -h option to the command 
    user@ubuntu:~$ free -h

         total     used    free   shared   buff/cache   available
Mem:     7.7Gi    675Mi   5.8Gi    179Mi        1.2Gi       6.6Gi
Swap:       0B       0B      0B

    Benefits of the free command in Linux

    The free command is an invaluable tool for managing and monitoring memory usage. In this section, we will delve into the benefits of utilizing this command.

    1. Display current memory usage
      Running the free command without any arguments will display the current amount of used and available memory on the system, as well as the amount of memory being used for system buffers and disk cache. This helps you to know processes eating up your systems storage.

    2. Monitor memory usage over time
      By using the free command in combination with the watch command, you can display the current memory usage at regular intervals. For example, "watch -n 5 free -m" will display the current memory usage every 5 seconds. This can be useful for identifying patterns in memory usage over time.

    3. Identify memory leaks
      If the "used" column of the free command output is consistently high, it may indicate a memory leak in one of the running programs. By running the command periodically and checking the used memory, you can identify the process that's causing the leak.

    4. Check for high buffer/cache usage
      If the "buffers" and "cached" columns are consistently high, it may indicate that the system is using a lot of memory for caching. While this is generally not a problem, it can cause slow performance if the system is low on memory.

    5. Display memory usage in different units
      By using the -m or -g options, you can display the memory usage in megabytes or gigabytes, respectively.

    6. Comparison with other command-line tools
      The free command can be used in conjunction with other command-line tools like top, htop, and vmstat, to provide a more complete picture of the system's memory usage and performance.

    Conclusion

    In this article, we discussed the free command, how it works and its benefits.

    The free command in Linux is a useful tool for monitoring the system's memory usage as well as a valuable tool for managing and optimizing the performance of Linux systems. Understanding the output of the free command can help administrators and users identify potential memory bottlenecks and troubleshoot performance issues.
item.author: danielfavour

item.title: 如何开始您的第一个开源贡献？？ | How to get started with your first Open Source Contribution??
item.description:
嘿开发人员，我们都知道从我们真正喜欢的其他人的想法中启动开发项目的冲动，我们经常将其称为Open Source Contribution 。 我们知道我们想要这样做，您的脑海中有很多关于该做什么的想法？ 以及使用什么？

但是，大多数人通常忘记了如何开始这个基本问题？ 首先。 我在开源开发的初始阶段也遇到过同样的困境。 所以，在这里我列出了一个简单的过程来开始你的第一个开源贡献。

以下是我在第一次开源贡献之前遵循的步骤，

步骤1：
学习和练习在您选择的 IT 职业领域中培养可能存在现实生活问题的任何基本技能。 第2步：
在Open Source Contributions中了解基本的git命令、 GitHub存储库及其用途。 在git-scm.com/doc中了解有关 git 的更多信息

第 3 步：
接下来，在 GitHub 社区中查找 GitHub 问题并选择一个。

您可以在GitHub search中搜索技术主题或关键字，在结果中您可以选择您喜欢的任何存储库，并查看存储库的问题部分以查找任何未解决的问题以供贡献。

或者，如果您在一年中的正确季节，您可能会参加Hacktoberfest Event （发生在 10 月，这是了解 git 和开源贡献的好地方）。

您可以先在 GitHub 搜索中使用#hacktoberfest2022或#hacktoberfest关键字进行搜索。

步骤4：
在第 3 步之后，开始在问题线程中与 GitHub 存储库所有者进行交互。 成为第一个互动并要求所有者将问题分配给您的人，以便您的贡献被保留以供考虑。

第 5 步：
在您找到要解决的问题后，开始解决它。 尽量满足问题的需要。

最后，尽量让自己对自己的发展感到满意，因为你投入的热情和努力会相互回报，让你拥有积极的心态。

一次，如果您完成了更改，请按照必要的 git 步骤进行操作并制作您的第一个 PR。

第 6 步：
创建 PR 后，不要忘记在问题线程中提及创建的 PR 详细信息，并更新请求代码所有者审查代码的线程。

等待审核。 如果您的代码设法解决了问题，您的更改将被接受并合并。

瞧！ 祝贺你！ 您已经做出了第一个开源贡献。

在实时页面中看到您的更改总是很高兴，您很幸运在合并更改后获得实时部署的回购协议。

我对第一个开源贡献的行动如下。

由于我的努力，代码所有者喜欢我的方法并批准了我的更改，并将其部署到实时页面。

有时，一个好的方法和对发展的热情会给你留下更多的机会。

我从同一个代码所有者和存储库中得到了更多问题。 我还对 UI 进行了改进，并为实时页面集成了 codeforces API。

这是我的更改部署后的实时页面 URL， codeforces-probem-of-the-day.netlify.ap ...

最后，一切都从第一步开始。 如果你来到这里，我希望你能了解你的第一步。 快乐编码！ 快乐开发！！

永远不要忘记感谢给你机会的人。 我想用这篇文章感谢@sceary-expert给我的第一次机会。



原文:

Hey Dev folks, we all knew the urge to kickstart a development project out of someone else idea which we really like and we often coin it as Open Source Contribution. We knew that we want to do it and there are plenty of thoughts wandering in your mind about what to do? and what to use?.

But, most people commonly forget the basic question of how to get started? in the first place. I was in the same dilemma in my initial stages of open source development. So, here I am listing out an easy process to start your first open source contribution.

Following are the steps that I have followed before my first open source contribution,

Step 1:
Learn and practice building any basic skills in the area of your choice of IT career that has potential real life problems. (I chose web development and there is always a scope to learn)

Step 2:
Learn about basic git commands, GitHub repositories and their purpose in Open Source Contributions. (If you know the reason, you will never forget it)

Learn more about git in git-scm.com/doc

Step 3:
Next, lookout for GitHub issues in the GitHub community and choose one.

You can search for technology topics or keywords in GitHub search, and in the results you can choose any repository to your likes and checkout the issues section for the repo for any open issues for contribution.

Or if you are in the right season of the year, you might end up in the Hacktoberfest Event (occurs in October, a great place to learn about git and open source contributions).

You can start by just searching using the #hacktoberfest2022 or #hacktoberfest keywords in the GitHub search.

(For more details on hacktoberfest, checkout opensource.com/article/22/10/how-to-con…)

Step 4:
After step 3, start interacting with the GitHub repository owner in the issue thread. (Always remember, early bird catches the fly. There might be other developers who are competing for the same issue)

Be the first to interact and ask the owner to assign the issue to you, so that your contribution is reserved for consideration.

Step 5:
After you got yourself an issue to work on, start working on it. Try to satisfy the needs of the issue.

Lastly, try to satisfy yourself with your development, because the passion and efforts you put in reciprocates each other and leaves you a positive mindset.

Once, if you are done with your changes, follow the necessary git steps and make your first PR.

Step 6:
After creating PR, do not forget mention the created PR details in the issue thread as well and update the thread requesting the code owner to review the code.

Wait for the review. If your code manages to pull off the issue, your changes will be accepted and merged.

Voila! and Congratulations!! You have made your first open source contribution.

It is always good to see your changes in live page, you are lucky to get a repo with live deployment after your changes are merged.

My Actions towards my first Open Source Contribution are as below.

Thanks to my efforts, the code owner liked my approach and approved my changes, also deployed it to the live page.

Sometimes, a good approach and passion for development leaves with you more opportunities.

I got more issues from the same code owner and repository. I have made UI improvements as well, along with codeforces API integration for the live page.

Here is the live page URL after my changes got deployed, codeforces-probem-of-the-day.netlify.ap…

In the end, it all starts with a first step. I hope you will get to know your first steps, if you came till here. Happy Coding! Happy Dev!!

And always do not forget to be grateful to the one who gave you the opportunity. I would like to use this post to thank @sceary-expert for the first opportunity.
item.author: satyakoidala

item.title: DevOps 测试策略的最佳实践和工具 | Best Practices & Tools for DevOps Testing Strategy
item.description:
软件开发公司一直在采用 DevOps，因为它有助于自动化和简化他们的应用程序开发生命周期。 不仅如此，DevOps 还通过规划、沟通、流程和工具更好地协调开发和运营团队，从而提高项目交付的质量和速度。

自 DevOps 发展以来，企业要么使用 Agile+DevOps，要么只选择 DevOps 方法。

但是测试 DevOps 的最佳策略是什么？ 为了帮助您，我们将讨论 DevOps 的基本概念、它的生命周期、最佳实践和您应该使用的工具。

什么是 DevOps？
在 DevOps 方法下，开发团队和运营团队并不是彼此孤立的。 这两个团队通常合并为一个团队，开发人员负责应用程序从开发到部署和运营的生命周期。 此外，DevOps 团队拥有广泛的技能，并不局限于应用程序的一种功能或特性。

阅读：DevOps 实际上做了什么

有时，安全和质量保证团队会在整个应用程序开发过程中与 DevOps 集成。 在这种情况下，DevOps 团队的重点是应用程序的安全性，也称为 DevSecOps。

与传统的手动实践不同，DevOps 团队使用技术堆栈和工具来自动化流程，以可靠、快速地构建应用程序。 此外，DevOps 工具允许开发人员独立执行配置基础设施或部署代码等任务，否则这些任务需要其他团队的协助。

开发运营生命周期
这个无限循环代表了持续的协作策略，其中包括适用于应用程序生命周期每个阶段的技术堆栈和工具。

生命周期的左侧部分处理应用程序开发和测试，而右侧描述部署和操作的周期。

计划：DevOps 团队确定业务需求并在计划阶段收集用户的反馈。 为了最大化业务价值并提供预期结果，开发人员还在此阶段创建项目路线图。

代码：在此阶段，创建应用程序代码并使用 Git 或 GitHub 等工具和插件简化流程，以最大限度地减少糟糕的编码实践和应用程序安全方面的缺陷。

构建：在构建阶段，开发人员使用 Gradle 或 Maven 等工具将代码提交到共享存储库。

测试：在测试阶段，将构建部署到测试环境，通过使用Selenium、JUnit等工具运行安全、用户接受度、集成、性能等不同测试来保证应用程序质量。

发布：当构建在通过测试后准备好部署到生产环境中时，发布由运营团队安排。

部署：在部署阶段，基础架构即代码使用不同的工具来构建和部署生产环境。

运营：一旦最终用户可以访问该版本，运营团队就会使用 Ansible、Saltstack、CFEngine 或 Chef 等工具进行服务器配置和供应。

通过环境监控，团队可以轻松识别影响生产力的瓶颈。

从敏捷到 DevOps
即使 DevOps 和敏捷测试之间存在细微差别，使用敏捷的个人可能会发现更熟悉 DevOps 并最终采用它。 尽管敏捷原则在开发和 QA 的迭代中得到了成功的应用，但在运营方面却没有那么成功。 这就是 DevOps 的用武之地。

使用 CD，可以非常频繁地开发、测试和发布软件应用程序。

随着流程和环境在 DevOps 中标准化，持续的开发过程使整个链条中的每个人都受益。 由于 DevOps 中的所有流程都是自动化的，它允许开发人员专注于设计和编码高质量的应用程序，而不是强调构建、质量保证和操作流程。

阅读：Salesforce DevOps 的好处和挑战

使用持续开发可显着缩短编写代码和承诺为用户部署生产之间的时间长达 4 小时。

简而言之，DevOps 是敏捷的扩展，或者可以称为“类固醇上的敏捷”。

值得庆幸的是，一些 DevOps 测试最佳实践可以被理解并用于任何应用程序开发。 尽管解释 DevOps 的每个测试最佳实践超出了本文的范围。 这就是为什么我们总结了 DevOps 测试的每一个最佳实践，并在下面进行了解释。

  1. 质量检查是管道阶段的一个重要方面，涉及所有团队成员。 此外，质量测试不能留给完全不同的团队进行。 因此，团队需要确定测试策略，以控制整个应用程序开发生命周期中测试活动的范围和数量。

阅读：在 Kubernetes 上运行有状态应用程序 - 最佳实践和用例

为了达到要求的结果，跨职能团队的每个成员都必须对测试及其结果负责。

鼓励围绕测试和测试结果分析进行协作，而不是测试人员和开发人员之间为代码修复而对抗。

领导者应将测试视为项目开发的战略部分，而不是将其视为可以降低的成本。 他们需要预算资金和时间来为 DevOps 团队中想要的开发人员提供测试培训资源、框架、工具、管理和创建评估策略。

开发团队应该参与测试创建和结果分析，而运营团队应该计划和执行跨职能测试。

  1. 持续测试策略 传统的瀑布式测试方法是在开发周期快结束时由独立的 QA 团队测试应用程序中的大量更改，这种方法无法与 DevOps 一起使用。

随着 DevOps 团队在持续交付管道的所有阶段测试微小的变化，敏捷方法与 DevOps 测试更加兼容。

阅读：什么是定制软件开发

虽然敏捷强调持续测试的重要性以及将持续测试集成到软件工具中的必要性，但它并未定义将测试扩展到部署的方法。 持续测试策略必须包括在管道和部署的所有阶段进行测试的集成。

  1. 端到端测试集成 DevOps 需要跨端到端管道阶段的测试水平集成以及跨不同级别的持续交付基础设施的垂直集成。

下面列出了实现端到端测试集成的最佳实践：

在集成之前使用私有实例对应用程序中的更改进行测试，以确保代码更改不会破坏分支。 这样做的一些测试方法是静态代码分析、单元测试、性能测试、回归、扫描和功能测试。

在预集成测试阶段，应创建自动化测试以用于管道中的后续测试阶段。

为了验证预集成测试的测试结果，DevOps 团队应该在提交代码时对代码进行评估。

在构建阶段，应执行测试以确定集成构建是否满足验收标准。

为了确保构建镜像的性能和功能满足评估标准，必须在代码测试过程中提交性能和功能测试。

同样，在回归、系统测试和交付等其他阶段，也需要进行一组测试，以确保代码和应用符合预期的评估标准。

  1. 为此，最佳实践是找到并包括基础设施即代码、动态基础设施配置工具、云服务和测试即服务，它们比专用基础设施更具成本效益和可行性，并且易于建立和发布运行测试所需的基础设施配置。

    1. 您可以使用的一些工具包括功能测试工具、协议测试工具、API 测试工具、单元测试工具、数据库模拟器、性能/负载测试工具和用户界面测试工具。

    测试工具可以是白盒工具、灰盒工具和黑盒工具。 测试工具应该能够将测试工具链和框架融合在一起，以便为 DevOps 做好准备。

    这将增加纵向或横向按需扩展的弹性，并匹配工作负载需求以及通过持续交付管道测试应用程序变更的能力。 可以通过 API 编排、扩展、调用、控制和监视 DevOps 就绪工具。 资源，采用快速失败测试设计技术、加速测试结果监控的测试框架配置和测试工具配置。

    1. 测试分析
      如果对连续测试结果的分析跟不上测试速度，不仅会增加要分析的结果数量，还会导致无法节省时间、大量混淆和忽视有价值的结果，从而减缓CI/CT 循环。 一些可用于匹配测试速度及其分析的技术包括测试结果分析器、仪表板或向框架添加分析工具。

    2. 微服务和容器
      从测试的角度来看，微服务架构需要验证每个服务合同与使用它的其他服务。 服务间的依赖和微服务的独立性都应该得到很好的测试。

    在网络上运行服务时，还需要验证可靠性和性能考虑因素。 如果微服务受到应用程序更改或依赖的微服务组的影响，则需要对其进行回归测试。

    容器提供了将测试资源打包在特殊容器中的可能性，以方便和不可变，以及测试更改的按需扩展。

    1. 数据库 DevOps 测试
      至关重要的是要有一个策略来测试和验证对数据库或使用数据库的应用程序的任何更改是否在整个持续交付管道中按要求执行。 此外，必须有可用于从生产中复制数据量的工具，以确保在部署前对生产数据集进行测试。

    2. 通过制定 DevOps 安全测试策略，可以更轻松地使应用程序免于漏洞、威胁和风险。 自动化测试
      为了消除持续集成带来的风险，必须添加测试自动化以提供快速的应用程序质量反馈。 使用 CI 进行自动化测试可以让团队测试新代码的迭代并将出错的可能性降到最低。

    以下是可用于 DevOps 测试策略的不同测试工具的列表。

    在软件开发生命周期中使用 DevOps 测试工具为开发和运营团队提供了多项优势。 它提供的一些好处是代码质量改进、快速和持续的反馈，以及增加应用程序上市时间，这有助于增加开发、运营和测试团队。

    单元测试工具
    通过单元测试，DevOps 团队可以单独检查应用程序的源代码以验证其功能。 即使在应用程序的初始开发阶段也可以进行单元测试。 它依赖于模仿应用程序功能的测试用例。 这些测试用例要么通过，要么失败，并向用户提供结果，以便他们调试代码。

    一些单元测试工具是专门为给定的编程语言设计的。 您可以使用的一些工具是 Mocha（用于 JavaScript）、EMMA（用于 Java）、Typemock（用于 .Net 和 C++）、Parasoft（用于 C 和 C++）和 SimpleTest（用于 PHP）。

    性能测试工具
    性能测试在 DevOps 的后期完成，即代码编写和集成时。 根据项目需求，性能测试工具会对应用程序进行压力、负载、容量、容量和恢复测试，以检查应用程序的性能以及如何从崩溃中恢复。

    使用工具进行性能测试的目的是在将系统发布给最终用户之前检测崩溃源并修改系统以达到最高效率。 一些可用于性能测试的工具是 Apache JMeter、k6、Watir、Predator 和 TestComplete。

    自动化测试工具
    自动化测试工具有助于自动运行测试、管理测试数据并使用结果来提高软件质量。 除了减少人为错误外，自动化测试工具还可以进行规模化评估。 连续测试工具
    持续测试是测试应用程序的过程，有助于在 DevOps 管道的每个阶段进行代码、功能和应用程序验证，以检测错误并最大限度地减少周转时间。

    结论

    由于所有正确的理由，DevOps 是为多个企业开发应用程序的理想解决方案。 然而，应用程序的成功和质量完全取决于 DevOps 团队为测试应用程序而制定的策略。

    我们希望本文能帮助您为当前或未来的项目制定 DevOps 测试策略。

    此外，如果您是想要开发 Web 应用程序或需要为其业务提供按需解决方案的企业主，只需与我们联系，您就可以聘请精通和经验丰富的开发人员！



原文:

What are the Best Practices & Tools for DevOps Testing Strategy? Software development companies have been adopting DevOps since it helps automate and streamline their application development life cycle. Not just that, but, DevOps also improves the quality and speed of the project deliveries by making the coordination between development and operations teams better through planning, communication, processes and tools.

Since the evolution of DevOps, businesses are either using Agile+DevOps or just opting for DevOps methodologies.

(Agile is an iterative process focused on collabs, feedback and rapid releases.)

But what’s the best strategy for testing DevOps? To help you out, we will discuss the basic concept of DevOps, its lifecycle, best practices and tools that you should go for.

What is DevOps?
DevOps is the set of tools, cultural philosophies, and practices that improves the ability of an organization to deliver projects at high velocity by automating and integrating processes between development and operation teams. DevOps accentuates cross-team collaboration & communication, team empowerment, and automation.

Under a DevOps methodology, the development team and operations team are not isolated from each other. These two teams are often merged as one team where developers work on the app’s lifecycle from development to deployment and operations. Besides, the DevOps team has a wide range of skills that isn’t limited to one function or feature of the application.

Read: What does DevOps actually do

Sometimes, the security and quality assurance teams get integrated with DevOps throughout the application development. In this case, where the focus of the DevOps team is on the security of the application, it is also referred to as DevSecOps.

Unlike traditional manual practices, the DevOps team uses a technology stack and tooling that automates the process to build applications reliably and quickly. In addition, DevOps tools allow developers to carry out tasks such as provisioning infrastructure or deploying code independently, which would have otherwise required assistance from other teams.

DevOps Lifecycle
DevOps lifecycle is an automated processes series within a continuous development lifecycle. DevOps lifecycle works on an iterative approach that’s the reason practitioners have symbolized it as an infinity loop. This infinity loop represents the continuous and collaborative strategy that includes technology stacks and tools for every stage in the lifecycle of an application.

The left part of the lifecycle deals with the application development and testing while the right side depicts the cycle of deployment and operations.

DevOps LifecycleLet’s get an overview of the working DevOps lifecycle.

Plans: The DevOps team identifies business needs and gathers feedback from users during the planning phase. To maximize the value to the business and provide the desired result, developers also create a roadmap of the project at this stage.

Code: In this stage, the application code is created and the process is streamlined by using tools and plugins like Git or GitHub to minimize lousy coding practices and shortcomings in application security.

Build: In the build stage, developers commit the code to the shared repository with tools like Gradle or Maven.

Test: In the test phase, the build is deployed to the test environment so that the application quality can be ensured by running different tests such as security, user acceptance, integration, performance, and more using tools like Selenium, JUnit, etc.

Release: When the build is ready to be deployed in the production environment after passing tests, releases are scheduled by the operations team.

Deploy: In the deployment stage, Infrastructure-as-Code uses different tools to build and deploy the production environment.

Operate: Once the release is accessible to the end-users, the operations team works on server configuring and provisioning using tools like Ansible, Saltstack, CFEngine, or Chef.

Monitor: As the name suggests, in the monitoring stage, the DevOps pipeline is monitored according to the collected information on application performance, user behavior, and so forth. With environment monitoring, teams can easily identify bottlenecks that affect productivity.

From Agile to DevOps
Even with subtle differences between DevOps and Agile testing, individuals working with Agile might find DevOps more familiar and eventually adopt it. Although the principles of Agile are successfully applied in the iterations of development and QA, it is not as successful on the operations side. This is where DevOps comes in.

DevOps has now replaced Continuous Integration with Continuous Development, in which teams work to develop applications in short cycles so the software can be automatically and reliably released anytime. Using CD, the software application can be developed, tested, and released at a great frequency.

As processes and environments are standardized in DevOps, the continuous development process benefits everyone in the entire chain. As all the processes in DevOps are automated, it allows developers to focus on designing and coding a high-quality application instead of stressing about builds, quality assurance, and operations processes.

Read: Salesforce DevOps benefits and challenges

Using continuous development dramatically reduces the duration between the time code is written and committed to deployment on production for users up to 4 hours.

In short, DevOps is an Agile extension or can be called "Agile on Steroids."

Best Practices For DevOps Testing
DevOps test engineers need to re-think software QA test strategies to align with pipeline stages from development to operations. Thankfully, several DevOps testing best practices can be understood and used for any application development. Although it is beyond the scope of this article to explain each of the test best practices of DevOps. That’s why we have summarized every best practice of DevOps testing and explained them below.

  1. DevOps Test Culture The testing culture of DevOps differs as the responsibility of providing high-quality applications is shared between cross-functional team members. The quality check is a crucial aspect of the pipeline phases and involves all the team members. Besides, quality testing cannot be left for the end of the pipeline by a completely different team. So, teams need to determine the test strategies to control the extent and volume of testing activities throughout the application development lifecycle.

Read: Running Stateful Applications on Kubernetes - Best Practices & Use Cases

To achieve the required results, each member of the cross-functional team has to take the responsibility for testing and its results.

DevOps Test Culture should include the following characteristics:

Encouragement of collaboration around testing and test results analysis instead of confrontations between testers and developers for code repairs.

Test coverage and creation terms have been consented to by the DevOps team.

Leaders should consider testing as a strategic part of the project development instead of seeing it as a cost that can be reduced. They need to budget money and time to provide test training resources, frameworks, tools, management and creating assessment policies for developers they want in the DevOps team.

Dev teams should embrace the test creation and result analysis, while the Ops teams should plan and execute cross-functional tests.

  1. Continuous Test Strategies The traditional waterfall approach for testing where an extensive volume of changes in the application is tested near the end of the development cycle by an independent QA team cannot work with DevOps.

As the DevOps team tests small changes over all the stages of the continuous delivery pipeline, agile methodologies are more compatible with DevOps testing.

Read: What is Custom Software Development

While Agile emphasizes the importance of continuous testing and the need to integrate continuous testing into software tools, it does not define the methods for extending tests to deployment. The continuous testing strategies required for DevOps are more defined than those for waterfall or Agile. Continuous testing strategies must include the integration of testing in all the phases of the pipeline and deployment.

  1. End-to-End Test Integration DevOps requires horizontal integration of tests across end-to-end pipeline stages as well as vertical integration across different levels of continuous delivery infrastructure.

The best practices to achieve end-to-end test integration are enlisted below:

Conducting tests on the changes in the application before integration using a private instance to ensure the code changes do not break the branch. Some of the testing methods to do so are static code analysis, unit testing, performance testing, regression, scanning and functional testing.

In the pre-integration testing stage, automated tests should be created for use in later testing stages in the pipeline.

To verify test results from the pre-integration test, the DevOps team should conduct assessments on the code at the time of committing code.

In the build stage, tests should be performed to identify whether the integrated build meets the acceptance criteria.

To make sure that the performance and functionality of the build images meet the assessment criteria, performance and functional tests must be committed during the code testing process.

Similarly, at other stages such as regression, system tests, and delivery, a set of tests needs to be conducted to ensure that the code and application meet the expected assessment criteria.

  1. DevOps Test Infrastructures The application that is being tested can have either monolithic, 3-tier, service-oriented or microservices architecture. DevOps testing practices emphasize the importance of conducting tests in a production-like environment which assures that the test can cover all the configurations of the application once deployed to production.

For that, a best practice is to find and include infrastructure-as-code, dynamic infrastructure configuration tools, cloud services, and test-as-a-service that are more cost-efficient and feasible than dedicated infrastructures and easily stand up and release infrastructure configurations as required to run tests.

  1. DevOps-Ready Test Tools The continuous delivery test tools must offer capabilities to operate tests on the applications and provide data from the results required to set the test verdict. Some of the tools you can use are functional test tools, protocol test tools, API test tools, unit test tools, database simulators, performance/load test tools, and user interface test tools.

Test tools may be white box tools, gray box tools and black box tools. Test tools should be able to blend the test toolchain and framework to be DevOps-ready.

This will increase the elasticity of scale on-demand vertically or horizontally, and match the workload demands as well as the capacity of testing changes in application going through the continuous delivery pipeline. DevOps ready tools can be orchestrated, scaled, invoked, controlled, and monitored from an API. Resources, employing fail-fast test design techniques, test framework configurations that accelerate test results monitoring, and test tool configurations.

  1. Test Analytics
    If the analysis of the results from continuous testing does not get kept up with the speed of testing, it can not only increase the number of results to be analyzed but also lead to no time savings, mass confusion and overlooked valuable results that will slow the CI/CT cycles. Some techniques that can be used to match the speed of the test and its analysis include test result analysers, dashboards or adding analysis tools to the frameworks.

  2. Microservices and Containers
    From the testing perspective, with microservices architecture comes the need to verify each service contract with other services using it. Both inter-service dependencies and the independence of microservices should be well tested.

It is also necessary to verify the reliability and performance considerations when operating services over a network. Microservices need to be regression tested if they were affected by application changes or a dependent microservices group.

Containers provide the possibility of packaging test resources in special containers for convenience and immutability, as well as scalability on demand for testing changes.

  1. Database DevOps Testing
    It is crucial to have a strategy to test and verify any changes to the database or application using a database are performing as required throughout the pipeline of continuous delivery. Additionally, there must be tools that can be used to replicate data volumes from the production to ensure that the tests are conducted on the production like datasets before deployment.

  2. DevOps Security Testing
    By strategizing DevOps Security testing, it becomes easier to make the application free from vulnerabilities, threats and risks. DevOps team can apply automated tools and tests in the development cycle to minimize downtime, vulnerabilities and security threats.

  3. Automate Tests
    To eliminate the risks that come with continuous integration, it is essential to add test automation that will provide quick application quality feedback. Indulging automation testing with CI allows teams to test iterations of new code and minimize the possibility of errors.

DevOps Testing Tools
What are the best tools for DevOps Testing Strategy? Here is the list of different testing tools that can be used for the DevOps testing strategy.

DevOps Teting Tools

Using DevOps testing tools in the software development life cycle offers several advantages to the development and operations team. Some of the benefits it offers are code quality improvement, fast and continuous feedback, and increased application time-to-market that helps in increasing development, operations and testing teams.

Unit Testing Tools
With unit testing, the DevOps team can examine the source code of the application individually to verify its functionality. Unit testing can be done even at the initial development stage of the application. It relies on test cases that imitate the functionality of the application. These test cases either pass or fail and provide results to the user so that they can debug the code.

Some unit testing tools are specifically designed for a given programming language. A few tools you can use are Mocha (for JavaScript), EMMA (for Java), Typemock (for .Net and C++), Parasoft (for C and C++), and SimpleTest (for PHP).

Performance Testing Tools
Performance testing is done at the later stages of DevOps, i.e when the code is written and integrated. According to the requirements of the project, performance testing tools will subject the application to stress, load, capacity, volume and recovery tests to check the performance of the application and how it recovers from an outrage.

The aim of using tools for performance testing is to detect the crash source and modify the system for peak efficiency before releasing it to end-users. A few tools that can be used for performance testing are Apache JMeter, k6, Watir, Predator, and TestComplete.

Automated Testing Tools
Automated testing tools help to run tests automatically, manage test data and use the result to improve the quality of the software. Apart from reducing human errors, automated testing tools also enable scaled evaluations. Automated tools in the CI/CD model trigger tests based on events.

Some of the tools that the DevOps team can use for automated testing are TestProject, Leapwork, Opkey, Selenium, Tosca, and Testsigma.

Continuous Testing Tools
Continuous testing is the process of testing applications that helps in code, feature and application validation at every phase of the DevOps pipeline to detect bugs and minimize the turnaround time.

A few examples of continuous testing tools that are used by the DevOps team include AppVerify, Appium, Docker, Bamboo, and Jenkins.

Conclusion

DevOps is the ideal solution for the development of applications for several businesses because of all the right reasons. However, the success and quality of the application completely depend on the strategy the DevOps team made for testing the application.

We hope that this article has helped you out in strategizing DevOps testing for your current or future projects.

Besides, if you are a business owner who wants to develop a web application or need an on-demand solution for their business, you can hire developers who are well versed and experienced in the same by simply getting in touch with us!

item.title: 类型错误：无法将序列乘以浮点类型的非整数 | TypeError: can't multiply sequence by non-int of type float
item.description:
您在编码时遇到过“TypeError: can't multiply sequence by non-int of type float”的错误信息吗？ 这可能是一种令人沮丧的体验，尤其是对于初学者而言。 在这篇博文中，我们将解释此错误消息的含义、原因以及解决方法。

错误信息是什么意思？

当您尝试将序列（列表或元组）乘以浮点类型的非整数时，会出现错误消息“TypeError: can't multiply sequence by non-int of type float”。 让我们看一下可能产生此错误的示例代码：

 sequence = [1, 2, 3] result = sequence * 2.5

您可能希望result为[1, 2, 3, 1, 2, 3] ，但 Python 会引发错误消息“TypeError：无法将序列乘以浮点类型的非整数”。

是什么导致了错误信息？

当您尝试将序列乘以浮点类型的非整数时，会出现错误消息。 这样做的原因是 Python 中没有定义序列乘以浮点数。 您只能将一个序列乘以一个整数。

如何解决错误信息？

有几种方法可以解决“TypeError: can't multiply sequence by non-int of type float”错误信息。

将浮点数转换为整数

最简单的解决方案是将浮点数转换为整数。 您可以使用int()函数来执行此操作。 这是一个例子：

 sequence = [1, 2, 3] result = sequence * int(2.5) print(result)

此代码将输出[1, 2, 3, 1, 2, 3] ，这是您所期望的。

使用循环重复序列

另一种解决方案是使用循环来重复序列。 这是一个例子：

 sequence = [1, 2, 3] n = 2.5 result = [] for i in range(int(n)): result += sequence print(result)

此代码还将输出[1, 2, 3, 1, 2, 3] 。

定义自定义乘法函数

您还可以定义一个自定义乘法函数，将一个序列乘以一个浮点数。 这是一个例子：

 def multiply_sequence(sequence, n): if isinstance(n, int): return sequence * n else: result = [] for i in range(int(n)): result += sequence return result sequence = [1, 2, 3] result = multiply_sequence(sequence, 2.5) print(result)

此代码还将输出[1, 2, 3, 1, 2, 3] 。

结论

如果您发现自己需要将一个序列乘以一个浮点数，您可以将浮点数转换为整数，使用循环重复序列，或定义自定义乘法函数。 我们希望这篇博文能帮助您理解此错误消息以及解决方法。

一旦暂停，coder9 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

解除暂停后，coder9 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

取消发布后，coder9 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 coder9 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是您可以用来标记 coder9 的操作：

降低 coder9 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 coder9 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Have you ever encountered the error message "TypeError: can't multiply sequence by non-int of type float" while coding? It can be a frustrating experience, especially for beginners. In this blog post, we will explain what this error message means, what causes it and how you can solve it.

What does the error message mean?

The error message "TypeError: can't multiply sequence by non-int of type float" is raised when you try to multiply a sequence (list or tuple) by a non-integer of type float. Let's take a look at an example code that can produce this error:

sequence = [1, 2, 3]
result = sequence * 2.5

You may expect result to be [1, 2, 3, 1, 2, 3], but instead, Python raises the error message "TypeError: can't multiply sequence by non-int of type float".

What causes the error message?

The error message is raised when you try to multiply a sequence by a non-integer of type float. The reason for this is that multiplication of a sequence by a float is not defined in Python. You can only multiply a sequence by an integer.

How to solve the error message?

There are several ways to solve the "TypeError: can't multiply sequence by non-int of type float" error message.

Convert the float to an integer

The simplest solution is to convert the float to an integer. You can do this by using the int() function. Here is an example:

sequence = [1, 2, 3]
result = sequence * int(2.5)
print(result)

This code will output [1, 2, 3, 1, 2, 3], which is what you expect.

Use a loop to repeat the sequence

Another solution is to use a loop to repeat the sequence. Here is an example:

sequence = [1, 2, 3]
n = 2.5
result = []
for i in range(int(n)): result += sequence
print(result)

This code will also output [1, 2, 3, 1, 2, 3].

Define a custom multiplication function

You can also define a custom multiplication function that multiplies a sequence by a float. Here is an example:

def multiply_sequence(sequence, n): if isinstance(n, int): return sequence * n else: result = [] for i in range(int(n)): result += sequence return result sequence = [1, 2, 3]
result = multiply_sequence(sequence, 2.5)
print(result)

This code will also output [1, 2, 3, 1, 2, 3].

Conclusion

The "TypeError: can't multiply sequence by non-int of type float" error message can be avoided by ensuring that you only multiply a sequence by an integer. If you find yourself needing to multiply a sequence by a float, you can convert the float to an integer, use a loop to repeat the sequence, or define a custom multiplication function. We hope this blog post has helped you understand this error message and how to solve it.

Vicente Antonio G. Reyes - Feb 13

Unflagging coder9 will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: 什么是核心模块？ | What are Core Modules?
item.description:
大家好，我是软件工程师Subham Maity 。 我也喜欢通过我的教程教别人如何编码。 我总是渴望学习新事物并与社区分享我的知识。

并想与大家分享。 您可以在我的网站codexam.vercel.app/docs/node/node1上找到这篇文章 [Better View]

您可以在github.com/Subham-Maity/node-js-full-st找到存储库...

如果你想了解我最新的项目和文章，你可以关注我：

  • 领英
  • 推特
  • 教程网站

核心模块是内置于 Node.js 中并随安装自动提供的模块。 这些模块包括 Node.js 的最低限度功能，并被编译到其二进制分发版中。 您可以使用 require 函数将这些模块加载到您的程序中

这些模块提供基本功能，如创建 HTTP 服务器 ( http )、处理文件系统 ( fs )、处理文件路径 ( path )、提供有关操作系统 ( os ) 的信息以及处理二进制数据 ( buffer )。

全局模块是安装在您的系统上而不是项目目录上的 Node.js 包。 这意味着它们可以在您本地计算机上的任何地方使用。无需将它们导入到您的项目中。

例如，如果全局安装nodemon包，则可以在计算机上的任何位置使用它。 您不需要将其导入到您的项目中。 您只需在终端中运行nodemon即可启动服务器。

 console.log("Hello World")

在 Node.js 中，非全局模块是指不属于 Node.js 核心且未全局安装的模块。 相反，它是在您的 Node.js 应用程序中本地创建的。 这些模块可以是单个文件或多个文件/文件夹的集合。 在使用它们之前，您需要将它们导入到您的项目中。

 const fs = require('fs') fs.writeFileSync('hello.txt', 'Hello World')

如果只想从模块导入特定函数，可以使用解构语法。 例如，如果你想从fs模块导入writeFileSync函数，你可以这样做：

 const { writeFileSync } = require('fs') writeFileSync('hello.txt', 'Hello World')

或者

const fs = require('fs').writeFileSync; fs('hello.txt', 'Hello World')
  • 如果你愿意，你可以在这里更改函数的变量名，如下所示：
 const abc = require('fs').writeFileSync; abc('hello.txt', 'Hello World')



原文:

Hi, I'm Subham Maity, a software engineer. I also enjoy teaching others how to code through my tutorials. I'm always eager to learn new things and share my knowledge with the community.

⚡ I recently wrote an article on What are Core Modules? and wanted to share it with you all. You can find the article on my website codexam.vercel.app/docs/node/node1 [Better View]

⚡ I also have a repository on GitHub where you can find all the code and projects related to this topic. You can find the repository at github.com/Subham-Maity/node-js-full-st…

❗ For a more in-depth look at this topic, including a detailed table of contents, check out the complete tutorial on my GitHub Repo

If you want to stay updated with my latest projects and articles, you can follow me on:

  • Github
  • Instagram
  • YouTube
  • LinkedIn
  • Twitter
  • Tutorial Website

Core modules are modules that are built into Node.js and come automatically with its installation. These modules include bare minimum functionalities of Node.js and are compiled into its binary distribution. You can load these modules into your program by using the require function

Some examples of core modules in Node.js include http, fs, path, os, and buffer among others. These modules provide basic functionalities like creating an HTTP server (http), handling the file system (fs), dealing with file paths (path), providing information about the operating system (os), and handling binary data (buffer) .

⭐ What is global modules ?



Global modules are Node.js packages that are installed on your system rather than your project directory. This means that they can be used anywhere on your local computer.There is no need to import them into your project.

For example, if you install the nodemon package globally, you can use it anywhere on your computer. You don’t need to import it into your project. You can simply run nodemon in your terminal to start the server.

console.log("Hello World")

console.log is a global module

⭐ What is Non-global modules ?

In Node.js, a non-global module refers to a module that is not part of the Node.js core and is not installed globally. Instead, it is created locally within your Node.js application. These modules can be a single file or a collection of multiple files/folders. And you need to import them into your project before you can use them.

const fs = require('fs')
fs.writeFileSync('hello.txt', 'Hello World')

fs (file system) is a non-global module and we use this module to create a file named hello.txt and write "Hello World" in it.

console.log("Our directory is " + __dirname)
//output : Our directory is C:\Users\Subham Maity\Desktop\Node.js\Node.js Tutorial

__dirname is a non-global module and we use this module to get the current directory path.

console.log("Our file name is " + __filename)
//output : Our file name is C:\Users\Subham Maity\Desktop\Node.js\Node.js Tutorial\index.js

__filename is a non-global module and we use this module to get the current file path.

⚡ It's recommended always const abc = require('abc') should be at the top of the file and not in the middle of the code but if you want to use it in the middle of the code then it will work but it's not recommended.

If you want to import only a specific function from a module, you can use the destructuring syntax. For example, if you want to import the writeFileSync function from the fs module, you can do it like this:

const { writeFileSync } = require('fs')
writeFileSync('hello.txt', 'Hello World')

or

const fs = require('fs').writeFileSync;
fs('hello.txt', 'Hello World')
  • here if you want you can change the variable name of the function like this : 
const abc = require('fs').writeFileSync;
abc('hello.txt', 'Hello World')
